DISCOVERY 302,DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET TWO. THE MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. CCP 2033.220(A) REQUIRES THAT A PARTY ADMIT "SO MUCH OF THE MATTER" AS IS TRUE "OR AS REASONABLY AND CLEARLY QUALIFIED BY THE RESPONDING PARTY," DENY THE MATTER, OR SPECIFY SO MUCH OF THE MATTER FOR WHICH THE PARTY LACKS SUFFICIENT INFORMATION OR KNOWLEDGE. WHEN A RESPONDING PARTY "GIVES LACK OF INFORMATION OR KNOWLEDGE AS A REASON FOR FAILURE TO ADMIT ALL OR PART OF A REQUEST FOR ADMISSION, THAT PARTY SHALL STATE IN THE ANSWER THAT A REASONABLE INQUIRY CONCERNING THE MATTER IN THE PARTICULAR REQUEST HAS BEEN MADE, AND THAT THE INFORMATION KNOWN OR READILY OBTAINABLE IS INSUFFICIENT TO ENABLE THAT PARTY TO ADMIT THE MATTER." CCP 2033.220(B). WHILE THE AUTHENTICITY OF THE DOCUMENTS REFERENCED IN RFAS 8 AND 9 IS CONTESTED, STATING THAT THE RESPONDING PARTY NEEDS A DISCOVERY RESPONSE FROM THE PROPOUNDING PARTY IS NOT NECESSARILY AN ADEQUATE REASONABLE INQUIRY. MOREOVER, THE OBJECTIONS DO NOT RAISE AUTHENTICITY. WHILE THE SEPARATE STATEMENT DID NOT INCLUDE THE OBJECTIONS, AND WAS THEREFORE IMPROPER UNDER CRC 3.1345, IN THE INTEREST OF RESOLVING THIS DISPUTE I EXERCISE MY DISCRETION TO CONSIDER THE MOTION. MILLS V. U.S. BANK (2008) 16 CAL.APP.4TH 871, 893. BASED ON THE SHORT RESPONSE TO THE MEET-AND-CONFER CORRESPONDENCE, I FIND THAT DEFENDANT YAN WAS NOT REQUIRED TO TRY TO DO MORE TO MEET-AND-CONFER BEFORE BRINGING THIS MOTION. REVISED RESPONSES TO RFAS 8 AND 9 DUE BY SEPTEMBER 13, 2019. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM SCOTT BORROWMAN, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI'S MOTION TO COMPEL THIRD PARTY EARLY WARNING SERVICES, LLC TO COMPLY WITH SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS. PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI' S MOTION TO COMPEL THIRD PARTY EARLY WARNING SERVICES, LLC TO COMPLY WITH SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE COURTESY COPY OF PLAINTIFF'S MOVING PAPERS DO NOT INCLUDE COPIES OF THE EWS SUBPOENA OR THE CONSUMER OBJECTIONS WITH HIS MOVING PAPERS, OR ANY OF THE OTHER ATTACHMENTS TO THE THAI DECLARATION, THUS THE COURT IS NOT ABLE TO NECESSARY REVIEW. FURTHER, THE PROOF OF SERVICE DOES NOT SHOW THAT THE MOVING PAPERS WERE SERVED ON ALL PARTIES TO THE ACTION. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING AND DROP A HARD PAPER COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX FOR THE COURT'S REVIEW AND SIGNATURE. DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR MANDATORY DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S CONSOLIDATED ACTION TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 TO BE HEARD ON MARCH 12, 2026
Off Calendar Mar-12-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's MOTION FOR MANDATORY DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S CONSOLIDATED ACTION TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 302 TO BE HEARD ON MARCH 12, 2026.

Defaulted Defendant Demas Yan's motion for mandatory dismissal of Plaintiff Charles Li's consolidated action is DENIED.

Defendant lacks standing to bring this motion because he is in default. Default was entered against Defendant on April 3, 2023. On January 27, 2026, this court denied Defendant's motion to set aside default. Defendants in default have no standing to file motions to dismiss without first obtaining relief from the default. (Brown v. Ridgeway (1983) 149 Cal.App.3d 732, 735.)

Plaintiff to to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Action
Off Calendar Jul-25-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion For Mandatory Dismissal Of Plaintiffs Action.

Defendant Yan's motion to dismiss is denied. Case CGC-18-571386 was consolidated with CGC-22-597421. Yan was defaulted in CGC-18-571386 on April 3, 2023, and plaintiff dismissed CGC-22-597421 as to Yan on July 2, 2025. Yan now seeks to dismiss CGC-18-571386 based on the mandatory 5-year period to bring a case a trial. (See CCP 583.310.) Yan has no basis to seek that relief since he is in default. (Sporn v. Home Depot USA, Inc. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1294, 1301 ["After the default was entered, defendant was no longer an active party in the litigation and thus was not entitled to any further notices. 'The clerk's entry of default cuts off the defendant's right to take further affirmative steps such as filing a pleading or motion, and the defendant is not entitled to notices or service of pleadings or papers.'"].) That Yan was not defaulted in CGC-22-597421, which is now dismissed, is presently irrelevant to this motion.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/DH)

Motion To Compel Airbnb Inc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Apr-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHARLES LI's Motion To Compel Airbnb Inc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents.

Plaintiff Charles Li's unopposed Motion To Compel Airbnb Inc To Comply With Subpoena For Production Of Documents is DENIED.

The proof of service does not show that the moving papers were served on all parties to the action. In fact, the proof of service attached to Plaintiff's MPA shows service of a different document unrelated to this motion.

The record indicates Plaintiff's personal service on AirBNB did not provide 16 court days notice of his motion. (See Rules of Court, rule 3.1346 ["A written notice and all moving papers supporting a motion to compel an answer to a deposition question or to compel production of a document or tangible thing from a nonparty deponent must be personally served on the nonparty deponent unless the nonparty deponent agrees to accept service by mail or electronic service at an address or electronic service address specified on the deposition record."]; Code Civil Procedure section 1005(b) ["Unless otherwise ordered or specifically provided by law, all moving and supporting papers shall be served and filed at least 16 court days before the hearing."].) Thus, the merits of the motion are not properly before the court.

Plaintiff is ordered to submit a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 18, 2025. Plaintiff shall send the proposed order by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Plaintiff is ordered to serve notice of entry of any order on this motion on all parties.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two
Off Calendar Sep-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No Appearances: The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN's Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two. The motion to compel is GRANTED. CCP 2033.220(a) requires that a party admit "so much of the matter" as is true "or as reasonably and clearly qualified by the responding party," deny the matter, or specify so much of the matter for which the party lacks sufficient information or knowledge. When a responding party "gives lack of information or knowledge as a reason for failure to admit all or part of a request for admission, that party shall state in the answer that a reasonable inquiry concerning the matter in the particular request has been made, and that the information known or readily obtainable is insufficient to enable that party to admit the matter." CCP 2033.220(b). While the authenticity of the documents referenced in RFAs 8 and 9 is contested, stating that the responding party needs a discovery response from the propounding party is not necessarily an adequate reasonable inquiry. Moreover, the objections do not raise authenticity. While the Separate Statement did not include the objections, and was therefore improper under CRC 3.1345, in the interest of resolving this dispute I exercise my discretion to consider the motion. Mills v. U.S. Bank (2008) 16 Cal.App.4th 871, 893. Based on the short response to the meet-and-confer correspondence, I find that Defendant Yan was not required to try to do more to meet-and-confer before bringing this motion. Revised responses to RFAs 8 and 9 due by September 13, 2019. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Scott Borrowman, Clerk K. Liu, not reported.
